vocab.meaning: khả năng cảm thụ cái đẹp/gu thẩm mỹ
vocab.example: Spending an afternoon at the Louvre truly refined my aesthetic sensibilities regarding Renaissance portraiture.

vocab.meaning: nghệ thuật tiên phong (phá cách, mới lạ)
vocab.example: The gallery featured several avant-garde installations that challenged conventional notions of space and form.

vocab.meaning: người am hiểu tường tận/người sành sỏi
vocab.example: I wouldn't call myself an art connoisseur, but I can certainly distinguish between different brushwork techniques.

vocab.meaning: gợi suy nghĩ/kích thích tư duy
vocab.example: The exhibition on climate change was deeply thought-provoking, leaving me to reflect on our ecological impact.

vocab.meaning: để lại/truyền lại (di sản, tác phẩm nghệ thuật)
vocab.example: Many of the masterpieces in the national gallery were bequeathed by private collectors to be enjoyed by the public.
I was immediately drawn to the landscape paintings because of the artist’s use of a vibrant palette.
It was fascinating to see how digital screens are becoming a mainstream artistic medium in contemporary galleries.

vocab.meaning: vẻ đẹp trang nhã/tinh tế không phô trương
vocab.example: The minimalist sculptures possessed an understated elegance that didn't overwhelm the small exhibition room.
Seeing such a famous masterpiece in person is a completely different experience from viewing it in a textbook.
Art galleries serve as vital repositories of our cultural heritage, preserving stories for future generations.
